finding: ContextGuard reduced uncached input and generated tokens, but total input and tool-output volume increased; elapsed time was effectively unchanged.
identified_problem: On Codex CLI 0.128.0, the ContextGuard hook scripts executed, but PreToolUse updatedInput and PostToolUse replacementOutput were not applied. An 80,573-byte failing pytest output therefore reached the optimized agent.
required_solution: Update and compatibility-test hook response envelopes against supported Codex CLI versions, then repeat the same A/B benchmark.
limitations: Single controlled sample; model execution is stochastic.
Raw ran first and ContextGuard second.
Cached input is reported separately because it dominates total input in both runs.
The result measures the current plugin behavior, including the discovered hook compatibility defect.
